channel.title: quant-ph (TEST FEED — 架空データ)
channel.description: テスト用フィード。実在しない論文データを含む。

item.title: [TEST/qec] A high-threshold decoder for surface codes under biased noise
item.description: arXiv:2606.99001 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: qec (誤り訂正・符号理論) We present a fault-tolerant decoding scheme for the surface code tailored to biased Pauli noise. Our decoder improves the code distance threshold and reduces the logical error rate compared with standard minimum-weight perfect matching, demonstrating improved performance for practical stabilizer codes under asymmetric noise models.
item.dc:creator: Alice Researcher, Bob Collaborator

item.title: [TEST/ft] Magic state distillation protocols with reduced overhead
item.description: arXiv:2606.99002 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: ft (フォールトトレラント計算) We introduce improved magic state distillation protocols that achieve reduced resource overhead for universal fault-tolerant quantum computation. Our lattice surgery based implementation performs logical gate sequences via code switching, significantly lowering the cost of magic state injection and improving the threshold for the concatenated code architecture.
item.dc:creator: Carol Faulttolerant

item.title: [TEST/algo] Geometric Algebra Quantum Gate Decomposition via Qubitization
item.description: arXiv:2606.99003 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: algo (量子アルゴリズム) We formulate the Pauli and Clifford groups within the complex Geometric Algebra (GA) framework and introduce a greedy Pauli rotor decomposition algorithm. Using quantum signal processing and qubitization, we achieve Clifford+T universality with unexpectedly compact decompositions. We further demonstrate quantum speedup over classical algorithms for the decomposition problem via amplitude amplification.
item.dc:creator: Dan Algorithm, Eve Speedup

item.title: [TEST/complexity] QMA-hardness of the k-local Hamiltonian problem with improved parameters
item.description: arXiv:2606.99004 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: complexity (量子複雑性理論) We prove improved QMA-hardness results for the local Hamiltonian problem, tightening the promise gap and extending the result to new complexity classes. Our oracle separation shows BQP is strictly contained in QMA under plausible query complexity assumptions. We further analyze the quantum PCP conjecture and establish new lower bounds on quantum circuit complexity.
item.dc:creator: Frank Complexity

item.title: [TEST/nisq] Barren plateau mitigation in variational quantum eigensolvers
item.description: arXiv:2606.99005 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: nisq (変分・NISQアルゴリズム) We analyze the barren plateau phenomenon in parameterized quantum circuits used for VQE and QAOA on noisy intermediate-scale quantum devices. We propose an error mitigation strategy combining zero-noise extrapolation with quantum natural gradient optimization to overcome vanishing gradients, demonstrating improved convergence on benchmark Hamiltonians.
item.dc:creator: Grace NISQ, Hank Variational

item.title: [TEST/sim] Trotter error bounds for digital Hamiltonian simulation of lattice gauge theories
item.description: arXiv:2606.99006 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: sim (量子シミュレーション) We derive tight Trotter error bounds for the digital quantum simulation of lattice gauge theories using product formula decompositions. Our LCU-based approach achieves near-optimal query complexity for time evolution of many-body systems, with direct applications to quantum chemistry first-principles calculations beyond the variational eigensolver paradigm.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Simulator

item.title: [TEST/qml] Quantum kernel methods for classification with provable dequantization bounds
item.description: arXiv:2606.99007 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: qml (量子機械学習) We study quantum neural networks and quantum kernel methods for supervised classification tasks. We establish learning-theoretic bounds for quantum support vector machines on quantum data and analyze conditions under which dequantization is possible. Our quantum feature map construction demonstrates advantages over classical kernel methods on structured datasets.
item.dc:creator: Jane QML, Karl Learning

item.title: [TEST/qit] One-shot quantum channel capacity via resource theory of coherence
item.description: arXiv:2606.99008 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: qit (量子情報理論) We derive one-shot bounds on quantum channel capacity and entanglement-assisted classical capacity using resource theory methods. Our approach connects squashed entanglement and von Neumann entropy through quantum relative entropy, yielding tight achievability and converse results for quantum Shannon theory beyond the i.i.d. regime.
item.dc:creator: Laura QIT

item.title: [TEST/network] Entanglement distribution rates over lossy quantum repeater networks
item.description: arXiv:2606.99009 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: network (量子ネットワーク・通信) We analyze entanglement distribution across a chain of quantum repeaters in a quantum internet setting. Using entanglement swapping and purification at memory nodes, we derive achievable rates for the quantum network and compare link-layer scheduling protocols for entanglement distribution across metropolitan-scale quantum routers.
item.dc:creator: Mike Network

item.title: [TEST/crypto] Device-independent QKD with improved noise tolerance via randomness expansion
item.description: arXiv:2606.99010 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: crypto (量子暗号・QKD) We propose a device-independent QKD protocol that tolerates higher noise via randomness expansion. Security is proved against general attacks using the entropy accumulation theorem. We further discuss quantum random number generation and quantum secret sharing as extensions, comparing our protocol with MDI-QKD and twin-field QKD in practical settings.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Crypto, Oscar QKD

item.title: [TEST/pqc] Tighter security proofs for CRYSTALS-Kyber under quantum random oracle model
item.description: arXiv:2606.99011 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: pqc (耐量子計算機暗号) We provide tighter security reductions for the CRYSTALS-Kyber post-quantum KEM based on the module learning with errors (MLWE) problem. Our analysis in the quantum random oracle model improves the concrete security estimates for NIST post-quantum standardization candidates including Dilithium and NTRU, contributing to quantum-safe cryptographic infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Paul PQC

item.title: [TEST/hardware] Benchmarking two-qubit gate fidelity in superconducting transmon processors
item.description: arXiv:2606.99012 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: hardware (量子ハードウェア・実装) We report calibration and crosstalk mitigation techniques that improve two-qubit gate fidelity in a superconducting transmon quantum processor. Randomized benchmarking and cross-entropy benchmarking confirm improved readout fidelity and reduced Josephson junction noise. Our results provide a pathway toward fault-tolerant implementation in solid-state quantum computing hardware.
item.dc:creator: Quinn Hardware

item.title: [TEST/sensing] Heisenberg-limited quantum metrology with spin-squeezed atomic clocks
item.description: arXiv:2606.99013 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: sensing (量子センシング・計測) We demonstrate entanglement-enhanced sensing beyond the standard quantum limit using spin squeezing in optical atomic clocks. Our protocol exploits quantum Fisher information to approach the Heisenberg limit in Ramsey spectroscopy, with applications to quantum magnetometry and quantum gyroscope development for inertial navigation.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Sensing

item.title: [TEST/foundations] Bell inequality violations and contextuality in weak measurement scenarios
item.description: arXiv:2606.99014 Announce Type: new Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: foundations (量子基礎・測定理論) We investigate Bell inequality violations under decoherence using weak measurement and quantum Zeno dynamics. Our analysis of the Lindblad master equation for open quantum systems reveals the relationship between contextuality and macrorealism, with implications for quantum thermodynamics and the Wigner's friend thought experiment in the quantum reference frame formalism.
item.dc:creator: Sam Foundations

item.title: [TEST/other] Strongly correlated electrons in twisted bilayer systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.99015 Announce Type: cross Abstract: 【テスト用データ・実在しない論文】期待分類: other (その他・異分野) — cond-mat.str-el が primary This condensed-matter paper studies strongly correlated electrons and superconductivity in twisted bilayer materials. It is cross-listed to quant-ph only tangentially. The primary category cond-mat.str-el should trigger category_other_overrides and route this paper to the other channel.
item.dc:creator: Tom Condensed
